title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is unusually concentrated on evaluation and governance mechanics rather than new model capabilities. The strongest updates focus on how frontier systems should be independently tested, governed against emerging regulation, and audited for harmful agentic behavior, alongside a few research papers on fairness, privacy, and safety-sensitive deployment.

items.title: OpenAI outlines a playbook for trustworthy third-party evaluations
items.why_it_matters: It pushes clearer norms for independent capability and safeguard testing of frontier models.

items.title: OpenAI publishes Frontier Governance Framework
items.why_it_matters: It links lab risk management practices to emerging EU and California AI compliance expectations.

items.title: Gram introduces automated alignment auditing for sabotage propensity
items.why_it_matters: It offers a concrete auditing method for detecting harmful agentic behavior in deployment-like scenarios.

items.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
items.why_it_matters: It targets fairness in privacy-sensitive distributed training, a key issue for real-world high-stakes AI.

items.title: Private analytics via zero-trust aggregation
items.why_it_matters: It advances privacy-preserving analytics by reducing trust assumptions around data aggregation.

items.title: LLUMI explores safer mental health writing assistance using community feedback
items.why_it_matters: It addresses safety and usefulness tradeoffs in a sensitive deployment domain without relying on proprietary systems.

items.title: OpenAI expands trusted access to GPT-Rosalind for biodefense
items.why_it_matters: It shows a controlled-access deployment model for high-risk bio capabilities and preparedness use cases.

items.title: OpenAI details election information and safeguards for 2026
items.why_it_matters: It updates platform-level measures for election integrity, transparency, and cyber defense support.

items.title: LLMSurgeon proposes post-hoc auditing of LLM data mixtures
items.why_it_matters: It could improve transparency into training data provenance that shapes model behavior and failure modes.

items.title: NIST expands AI consortium scope and seeks new members
items.why_it_matters: It may shape shared measurement and evaluation infrastructure used across AI governance and assurance efforts.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
